title: Sorbus khasiana
taxonomy: Sorbus khasiana is a species of rowan native to the Himalayas. It belongs to the family Rosaceae. The genus Sorbus is a group of deciduous trees and shrubs that are commonly known as rowans or mountain ashes. The species Sorbus khasiana is characterized by its small white flowers and red berries. It is often classified as a type of flowering plant within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Sorbus khasiana. Limited information is available on the medicinal uses of Sorbus khasiana. However, in traditional Tibetan medicine, the plant is used to treat various health conditions. benefits: Sorbus khasiana, also known as the Kashmir rowan or Kashmirian mountain ash, has been reported to have various health benefits. Its extracts have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. intro: Sorbus khasiana, also known as the Kashmirian rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, helping to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Sorbus khasiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and to support overall well-being.
